urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Foreign-born population by age group and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on the foreign-born population by age group and sex for Türkiye total since 2014. Age groups are given as broad age groups (0-14, 15-64, 65+).

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Foreign-born population by place of birth and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on the foreign-born population by sex for Türkiye total since 2014.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by place of birth and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual information on the foreign-born population by sex at the place of birth level since 2014. Place of birth is categorized as "province of birth", "abroad" and "unknown".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by province of residence and place of birth
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on population by province of residence and place of birth since 2014. Place of birth is categorized as "province of birth", "abroad" and "unknown".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Number of households by type and provinces
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual information on the total number of households and the number of households by type at the provincial level since 2014. Household is defined as a person living alone or a group of persons residing in the same address, with or without any kind of kinship. Persons living in collective living quarters (institutional place, worker dormitories, guesthouses, hotels etc.) are excluded. This definition is based on the international household-dwelling concept. Household types are broadly categorized as "One-person households", "One-family households", "Extended-family households" and "Multi-person no-family households".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Number of households by size and type
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on the number of households by type and size for Türkiye total since 2014. Household is defined as a person living alone or a group of persons residing in the same address, with or without any kind of kinship. Persons living in collective living quarters (institutional place, worker dormitories, guesthouses, hotels etc.) are excluded. This definition is based on the international household-dwelling concept. Household types are broadly categorized as "One-person households", "One-family households", "Extended-family households" and "Multi-person no-family households". In addition, one-family households can be further categorized as "Couple without resident children", "Couple with at least one resident child" and "Lone parents with at least one resident child". Furthermore, lone parents with at least one resident child can be further categorized as "Lone fathers with at least one resident child" and "Lone mothers with at least one resident child".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Number of one-family households by type and provinces
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual information on the number of one-family households by type at the provincial level since 2014.Household is defined as a person living alone or a group of persons residing in the same address, with or without any kind of kinship. Persons living in collective living quarters (institutional place, worker dormitories, guesthouses, hotels etc.) are excluded. This definition is based on the international household-dwelling concept. One-family households are categorized as "Couple without resident children", "Couple with at least one resident child" and "Lone parents with at least one resident child". Furthermore, lone parents with at least one resident child can be further categorized as "Lone fathers with at least one resident child" and "Lone mothers with at least one resident child".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Province of civil registration by province of residence
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on population by province of residence and province of civil registration since 2008. Foreign population are not covered in the table.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by province, marital status and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table shows the legal marital status of the population aged 15+ by year and sex for provinces as of 2008. Legal marital status classification is as "never married, married, divorced, widowed and unknown".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Foreign population by provinces and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on foreign population by province of residence and sex since 2007. Foreign population in the ABPRS covers individuals who are holding a valid residence/work permit at the reference day; and individuals who have a valid address declaration at the reference day while holding an identity document equivalent to residence permit such as international protection identity document; and the individuals who have already renounced his/her TR citizenship while having a valid address declaration at the reference day. In addition to Syrians under temporary protection, foreigners holding visas or residence permits shorter than 90 days with the purpose of training, tourism, scientific research, etc. are not covered.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Foreign population by country of citizenship
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on foreign population by country of citizenship (nationality) and sex for the last two years. Foreign population in the ABPRS covers individuals who are holding a valid residence/work permit at the reference day; and individuals who have a valid address declaration at the reference day while holding an identity document equivalent to residence permit such as international protection identity document; and the individuals who have already renounced his/her TR citizenship while having a valid address declaration at the reference day. In addition to Syrians under temporary protection, foreigners holding visas or residence permits shorter than 90 days with the purpose of training, tourism, scientific research, etc. are not covered.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population of province/district centers and towns/villages by province and sex by province
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual province-level information on population density, total population size, provincial/district centers and town/village populationby sex since 2007.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population of province/district centers and towns/villages by age group and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on population size of provincial/district centers and town/village by age group and sex for Türkiye total as of 2007. Age groups are given in five-year intervals up to 90+.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by province, age group and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on population size of the provinces by age group and sex as of 2007. Age groups are given in five-year intervals up to 90+.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population, annual growth rate of population, number of provinces, districts, towns, villages, and population density
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on the annual population, population growth rate, population density, and number of localities by type for Türkiye total since 2007. In the calculation of annual population growth rate for the related period, administrative division of the latest year is taken into consideration. Population density is defined as the population per square kilometre and is calculated by division of the population size by the surface area in square kilometres. In the calculation of population density indicator, the land surface area excluding “inland water bodies (natural and dam lakes, rivers and continental shelves, etc.)” is used in accordance with the international definitions. Please read the notes for the sharp increases or decreases in the table.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Number and population of municipalities
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on the number of municipalities by type and the total municipal population for Türkiye total as of 2007. Please read the notes for the sharp increases or decreases in the table.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Age dependency ratio
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual total, child, and elderly dependency ratios for Türkiye total since 2007. Total age dependency ratio indicates the total number of children and elderly people per person in working age. Child dependency ratio indicates the total number of children per person in working age. Elderly dependency ratio indicates the number of elderly people per person in working age.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population density by provinces
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual province-level information on population density since 2007. Population density is defined as the population per square kilometre and is calculated by division of the population size by the surface area in square kilometres. In the calculation of population density indicator, the land surface area excluding “inland water bodies (natural and dam lakes, rivers and continental shelves, etc.)” is used in accordance with the international definitions.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by marital status and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table shows the legal marital status of the population aged 15+ by year and sex for Türkiye total as of 2008. Legal marital status classification is as "never married, married, divorced and widowed".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population of province/district centers, towns/villages by provinces and districts
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains provincial and district level information on total population and population of provincial/district centers and towns/villages for the last year.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Median age by provinces and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual median age information by sex for provinces as of 2007. The median age is the age of the person in the middle when the ages of all people in the population are sorted from the new-born baby to the oldest.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Age dependency ratio by provinces
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes total/child/elderly dependency ratios at the provincial level since 2007. Total age dependency ratio indicates the total number of children and elderly people per person in working age. Child dependency ratio indicates the total number of children per person in working age. Elderly dependency ratio indicates the number of elderly people per person in working age.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Average size of households by provinces
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual information on average household size at the provincial level since 2008. Household is defined as a person living alone or a group of persons residing in the same address, with or without any kind of kinship. Persons living in collective living quarters (institutional place, worker dormitories, guesthouses, hotels etc.) are excluded. This definition is based on the international household-dwelling concept. Average household size is obtained by dividing the total household population by the total number of households.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population of province/district centers and towns/villages by years and sex, Population Censuses - ABPRS
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on the population of province/district centers and towns/villages by sex for Türkiye total starting from 1927. Information for the 1927-2000 period is provided based on the traditional population censuses of which the first one was conducted in 1927, subsequent ones were conducted in every five years between 1935 and 1990, and the last one was in 2000. Annual information from 2007 onwards is from the Address Based Population Registration System (ABPRS) results.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by years, age group and sex, Population Censuses - ABPRS
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on population size and proportions by broad age group and sex for Türkiye total starting from 1935. Broad age groups are "0-14, 15-64, and 65+." Information for the 1935-2000 period is provided based on the traditional population censuses of which were conducted in every five years between 1935 and 1990, and the last one was in 2000. Annual information from 2007 onwards is from the Address Based Population Registration System (ABPRS).

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Median age by years and sex, Population Censuses - ABPRS
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains annual median age information by sex for Türkiye total as of 1935. The median age is the age of the person in the middle when the ages of all people in the population are sorted from the new-born baby to the oldest. Information for the 1935-2000 period is provided based on the traditional population censuses of which were conducted in every five years between 1935 and 1990, and the last one was in 2000. Annual information from 2007 onwards is from the Address Based Population Registration System (ABPRS).

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Annual growth rate of population and population density of provinces by years
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on the annual population growth rate and population density for provinces since 2007. In the calculation of annual population growth rate for the related period, administrative division of the latest year is taken into consideration. Population density is defined as the population per square kilometre and is calculated by division of the population size by the surface area in square kilometres. In the calculation of population density indicator, the land surface area excluding “inland water bodies (natural and dam lakes, rivers and continental shelves, etc.)” is used in accordance with the international definitions.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population of provinces by years
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes infromation on the population size of provinces since 2000. End of the year populations for the 2000-2006 period were estimated based on the ABPRS results. Total numbers may not give the exact total due to rounding. Annual information from 2007 onward is from the Address Based Population Registration System (ABPRS) results.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Sex ratio by age group
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains information on the sex ratio by age group for Türkiye total as of 2007. Age groups are given in five-year intervals up to 90+. Sex ratio is defined as the number of males per 100 females.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: The most used male names by the year of birth and order in the first hundred names
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table provides information on the name order of alive males according to their year of birth based on the ABPRS records dated 31st December. Table is sorted alphabetically. For example, according to the 2024 ABPRS, when the names of men born in 2000 and who are still alive are sorted, the name "Mehmet" is located in the first order. For the same year, the name “Yusuf” is in the fourth order.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: The most used female names by the year of birth and order in the first hundred names
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table provides information on the name order of alive females according to their year of birth based on the ABPRS records dated 31st December. Table is sorted alphabetically. For example, according to 2024 ABPRS, when the names of women born in 2000 and who are still alive are sorted, the name "Zeynep" is located in the first order. For the same year, the name “Elif” is in the fourth order.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Periodic population size and proportion by age group and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains periodic(quarterly) information on population size and proportion by age group and sex for Türkiye total as of July 1, 2024. Age groups are given in five-year intervals up to 90+. Periodic population statistics based on the ABPRS are produced for Türkiye total for the reference dates of April 1, July 1, and October 1, respectively, and are published with the ABPRS results dated December 31. Q1 refers to the April 1st, Q2 refers to the July 1st, Q3 refers to the October 1st and Q4 refers to the December 31st of the reference year.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population of provinces by broad age groups
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table includes annual total population, population by broad age groups at the provincial level since 2007. Broad age groups are "0-14, 15-64, and 65+".

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by urban-rural classification, age group and sex
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description:
The table contains annual information on population size by age group, sex and urban-rural classification for Türkiye total as of 2022. Age groups are given in five-year intervals up to 90+.  The new urban-rural classification put forward by the European Statistical Office and categorized as "densely populated, intermediate-density and thinly populated" is based on the "Degree of Urbanisation (DEGURBA)". The classification shows the rural-urban class of an area based on population density over 1 km2 population grids. As data sources, Spatial Address Registration System (SARS) and Address Based Population Registration System (ABPRS) dated 31 December were used.
According to the classification, densely populated areas are defined as quarters and villages that have at least 50 % of their population living in urban centre grids. Intermediate density areas are quarters and villages that do not meet the criteria of densely populated areas and thinly populated areas. Thinly populated areas are quarters and villages that have more than 50% of their population living in rural grid cells. Following the determination of rural-urban class of quarters and villages, population of provinces and districts according to the new urban-rural classification are obtained through aggregation.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.name: Population by urban-rural classification, sex and provinces
urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description:
The table contains annual information on population size of the provinces by sex and urban-rural classification as of 2022. Age groups are given in five-year intervals up to 90+.  The new urban-rural classification put forward by the European Statistical Office and categorized as "densely populated, intermediate-density and thinly populated" is based on the "Degree of Urbanisation (DEGURBA)". The classification shows the rural-urban class of an area based on population density over 1 km2 population grids. As data sources, Spatial Address Registration System (SARS) and Address Based Population Registration System (ABPRS) dated 31 December were used.
According to the classification, densely populated areas are defined as quarters and villages that have at least 50 % of their population living in urban centre grids. Intermediate density areas are quarters and villages that do not meet the criteria of densely populated areas and thinly populated areas. Thinly populated areas are quarters and villages that have more than 50% of their population living in rural grid cells. Following the determination of rural-urban class of quarters and villages, population of provinces and districts according to the new urban-rural classification are obtained through aggregation.

urn:sdmx:org.sdmx.infomodel.datastructur.description: The table contains provincial and district level information on annual population growth rate for the last year.
